An Introduttion to Nativities. Boe Children, I leave to the judgaient.of, &. This Nativity is precifely rectified by Accidents, both; by thofe depcnd:ng upon the Dire&tions of Meuium Celi, and of the afcendant to their — re{fpective Promittors. fim ‘

There may happily be many more Authors extant who have written of Afrologie, bet no more have as yet come unto my hands ; thefe] mention are all my own,Xc. many of thefe perhaps have been fince printed at other places : ladeed Ptolomey hath been printed in folio, in quarto, in octavo, in fixteens, and hath been cranf{lated feverall times out of Greck into Latin: that lately printed at Leiden | conceive to be the moft exact, it was performed by Avatius, &c. In a word, fome may blame methat - I write in the Englifhb tongue; yec I truft I have offended no man, fith I write in my owne Language; and to fuch as {peal as] {peak ; nor doe! know that it is forbid unto man to write in his owne Language, or is any-man bound to read or heare - QOv000 2 , that
that contenteth him not: If this Book doe generally pleafe, ¥ fhall.account him good, and think him worthy tolive 5 but if he difpleafe, I then beleeve the memory of it fhall {oon perith, and my felfe hall count it ill: If notwithftanding this, mine Accufers will not be fatisfied with this common judgment, let them contene themfelves with the judgment of the time, which at length difcovereth the privy faulcs-of every thing :-which becaufe it is the father of truth, it gives judgment without paf- fion, and acctiftometh .evermore to pronounce true fentence of © the life or death of Writings. Finis 3 Deo gloria. Octob. 1. 16478.
